posts.content: In this context, I define world modeling as predicting the next plausible world state (or a longer duration of states) conditioned on an action.

posts.content:
From a pure "do good for the world" mission perspective, having the acting like a solid personalized tutor is one of the better uses of AI.

If OpenAI cares about the mission of making the world a better place, tutoring should be an area of investment, not one to silently remove.
posts.highlights: AI as a personalized tutor is a high-impact use case; OpenAI should invest in tutoring.
posts.worthReading: Highlights a key ethical and practical application of AI in education.

posts.highlights: References Searle's Chinese Room argument to discuss AI understanding.

posts.content:
This is going to get even worse as people realize that careful tuning in their prompts can make AI writing seem not like AI writing to readers.

We expect word counts to align, in some way, with thinking & value. Writing took effort. We are not mentally ready for the alternative.
posts.highlights: Prompt tuning can make AI writing indistinguishable from human writing, challenging our assumptions about effort and value.
posts.worthReading: Highlights an emerging challenge in AI-generated content detection.
